answer:
Gorgias supports two authentication methods depending on your app type [1]. 

For private apps, use HTTP Basic Authentication with your email address as the username and your API key as the password [2][3]. The header must contain a base64-encoded string of the credentials [4].

```curl
curl --request GET \
  --url https://your-customer-account.gorgias.com/api/account \
  --header 'Authorization: Basic base64encode(USERNAME:API_KEY)'
```

For public apps, you must use OAuth2 [1][4]. This method uses Bearer tokens provided after a user authorizes your app [5][6].

```curl
curl --request GET \
  --url https://your-customer-account.gorgias.com/api/account \
  --header 'Authorization: REDACTED_EXAMPLE'
```

You can generate API keys within your account settings under REST API [4][2].

results.title: Access your Gorgias REST API credentials
